This is especially important for government officials. **Now we have to ask ourselves as a country: Do we really want to elect someone who incited an insurrection and tried to overturn the last election... as our next Commander in Chief? Of course not.** What happens in this trial will have major implications on the next election. Even a good number of Republican politicians say they will refuse to vote for Trump if he is convicted of these crimes. clean_body: What do you say? We would love to meet you. | | | Peter, We just launched a contest to give supporters like you a chance to spend an evening with President Obama, President Clinton, and me. It’s a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to meet not one, not two, but THREE presidents! (And my campaign will cover the costs of your flight and hotel.) If that’s all you need to hear, use this link to be automatically entered for a chance to meet me, Barack, and Bill: People ask me all the time if these messages asking for $25 really matter in a presidential election that will cost so much money to win. Yes. They do. Grassroots supporters like you who give to these messages are the backbone of my campaign. So when you see our ads on TV and our numbers rising in the polls, your support is a big reason for that success. Today your donation also gets you and a guest a chance to meet and take a photo with three presidents. | If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | Hope to see you soon! Joe Joe Biden President of the United States

| [S.B. 44](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBUTl6F4a7HeO1k0FrbqBrdKPNWkTsf9ApGoHuBjd4ha0cETBiF3XR72Ed2SdCtqBLf2lfDD3F3X7Yt33rH2_QISysrqr-YkFXYmn--i5B-S5SW3vGroRcDx3v-k4PEYD0jeyyU1LGMtEdUwQLOJPFJ0=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patrons: VanValkenburg, Ebbin Owner of firearm; use of firearm by minor in an unlawful manner or to cause bodily injury; penalties. Creates a Class 1 misdemeanor for an owner of a firearm, as defined in the bill, who (i) allows a minor to possess such firearm or (ii) knows or reasonably should know that a minor is in close proximity, as defined in the bill, to such firearm as to allow such minor to possess or transport such firearm in violation of law and such minor (a) uses such firearm in an unlawful manner; (b) possesses such firearm within any school zone or within any building or property deemed a gun-free zone by a school board; or (c) in the case of an owner of a firearm who allows a minor to possess such firearm, uses such firearm to intentionally or with gross negligence cause bodily injury to himself or another person or in the case of an owner of a firearm who knows or reasonably should know that a minor is in close proximity to a firearm, uses such firearm to cause bodily injury to himself or another person. The bill elevates the penalty to a Class 5 felony if such owner of a firearm knows or reasonably should know that such minor has been charged with, convicted of, or adjudicated delinquent of a violent juvenile felony or has been the subject of a school-initiated threat assessment that has classified the risk posed by the minor as moderate, high, or eminent.
[S.B. 88](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBSakZ7k06GS1VWOPEsAcDQofKRk3YQ5LnLXNU-N6ZYfi1lKK82YnTCVhc5-fsSsbgwdQbg9Uv2uyB7MHLsHcVUqUreEijeWiOfGjPOeladFQ2zQmaeWAJo9JqK-UOcIc7VPbRBby66AZtCksjbshwY4=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Locke Decertification of law-enforcement officers and jail officers. Makes various changes to the provisions related to decertification of law-enforcement officers and jail officers. The bill provides that the Department of Criminal Justice Services, rather than the Criminal Justice Services Board as provided under current law, shall oversee decertification proceedings, including receiving notice of an event requiring decertification from the sheriff, chief of police, or agency administrator or their designee, immediately decertifying a law-enforcement officer or jail officer upon receiving such notice, and initiating a review of the decertification upon request by the decertified officer. The bill specifies that the Department shall initiate such a review of the decertification in accordance with the provisions of the Administrative Process Act. The bill provides that the findings and decision of the Department may be appealed to the Board and that the final administrative decision of the Board may be then appealed and reviewed by a court.
[S.B. 196](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBSakZ7k06GS1ihj57Evrc-zzsGZ9XmYzRyKSEZarX67SHGXra1dTHX3dqxR5fSOAIrTZbcivtZrP-iDfhR9fksu682idb2pNsyyH9j3hobrNUHmr83KOQGxDWVqqMEGtXn0QCUn8ukjJEEt0EMt6GeA=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: VanValkenburg Voter registration; list maintenance data standards; challenges to a voter's registration. Prohibits the use of voter data received from another state or jurisdiction or through a list comparison for list maintenance purposes when the data file does not include a unique identifier for each individual whose information is contained in the data file. The bill requires the Department of Elections to annually audit all sources of data utilized for list maintenance activities for the purpose of determining the validity, completeness, accuracy, and reliability of the data received from each source, and to include the results of such audit in its annual report to the Committees on Privileges and Elections regarding its list maintenance activities. Lastly, the bill provides that the Circuit Court of the City of Richmond has jurisdiction over any proceeding challenging a voter's registration. The bill includes technical amendments.

Authorizes the use of a trackable correspondence equivalent to certified mail for sending invoices for unpaid tolls to a vehicle owner prior to the Department of Motor Vehicles placing a registration stop on such vehicle due to unpaid tolls. Current law requires such invoices to be mailed by certified mail.

[S.B. 327](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBUTl6F4a7HeOjEeHicsmt48pU1IRpJUDjkYIO6n_sphQC6OZsONePxVsnN5me3mYtIF46LS9YCwo23TbbwDUwURoCc_KCC9qrhYi7bQoMOAhzhewPoIVnVbCQnW2gTY5clZYckLc0mxWL2rFqocq7DA=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Salim Purchase of firearms; age requirement; penalty. Prohibits any person under 21 years of age from purchasing a firearm, with exceptions for the purchase of a rifle or shotgun by a law-enforcement officer, correctional officer, jail officer, or member of the Armed Forces of the United States, the Virginia National Guard, or the National Guard of any other state. Accordingly, the bill prohibits a licensed dealer from selling, renting, trading, or transferring from his inventory a firearm to any person under 21 years of age, with the same exceptions. A violation of either prohibition is a Class 6 felony. The bill also expands the definition of "assault firearm" as the term applies to criminal history record information checks.

[S.B. 383](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBUTl6F4a7HeOwDfMEiPDKkH-a5Kxl44uVLmvGuKV0SPw_NVDHqN6_KHb3X798tw8l65XotnhD4FgriDHwsxGrvR2LP84f6PeryqmQ3l-FPMC8roY4XwgQEDkNV4NbVSsnvI0XgybO_X-p9Q157zksBA=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Deeds Carrying a firearm or explosive material within Capitol Square or building owned or leased by the Commonwealth; exemptions; public institutions of higher education; penalty. Limits the exemption from the prohibition on the carrying of any firearm or explosive material within any building owned or leased by the Commonwealth or agency thereof or any office where employees of the Commonwealth or any agency thereof are regularly present for the purpose of performing their official duties that currently applies to any property owned or operated by a public institution of higher education to instead apply to any individual within a building owned or operated by a public institution of higher education who possesses a weapon as part of such public institution of higher education's curriculum or activities or as part of any organization authorized by such public institution of higher education to conduct its programs or activities within such building.
[S.B. 428](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBfYswDuUsAv0UcTT2_hdbN80QxeYtEovlcjQylMon7v6L2dzjTL-fhO0dV1PgUG_lcQgEX4NwvzV8eFwNO_exDo9hRWimlx64zzZHrLdgtcQRV7xAUUYoZxF9sFEcpaBPML4SSATdNbnQOtIQfUfvQE=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: VanValkenburg
Elections; conduct of election; ranked choice voting; locally elected offices; report. Allows elections for any local or constitutional office to be conducted by ranked choice voting. Under current law, only elections of members of a county board of supervisors or a city council are allowed to be conducted by ranked choice voting. The bill also clarifies requirements for conducting elections using ranked choice voting and requires results for elections conducted by ranked choice voting to be reported along with other results reported on election night, except that such results must clearly be identified as preliminary and based on the first rankings in a ranked choice voting election. The bill provides that final tabulation for an election for a local or constitutional office that is not shared by more than one county or city is required to be conducted on the same day as other results are canvassed by the local electoral board and that final tabulation for and election for a local or constitutional office that is shared by more than one county or city is required to be conducted at a centralized facility under the supervision of the Department of Elections. The bill specifies that ranking data is required to be made publicly available by the Department and requires the State Board of Elections to provide standards and to approve vote tabulating software for use with existing voting systems in elections conducted by ranked choice voting. The bill also limits a risk-limiting audit of an election conducted using ranked choice voting to the first choice rankings reported on voting systems. Finally, the bill directs the Department to review the testing and approval framework for voting equipment in the Commonwealth and submit a report of such review no later than the first day of the 2025 Regular Session of the General Assembly.
[S.B. 491](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBUTl6F4a7HeOZp9vpGmRKbg9KcJ_pHu5cxAnQnzaRJE7C5m2mhHXzGX_Hbnb_Y8ZTSwQES9otlpSzKL-kHybdGQv4JTD3JxXbM6yd4QFjh3rSCSqbTZti1y46xx4__QTag08CvC956Av2uFNcU4DYx0=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Carroll Foy
Firearm industry members; standards of responsible conduct; civil liability. Creates standards of responsible conduct for firearm industry members and requires such members to establish and implement reasonable controls regarding the manufacture, sale, distribution, use, and marketing of the firearm industry member's firearm-related products, as those terms are defined in the bill. Such reasonable controls include reasonable procedures, safeguards, and business practices that are designed to (i) prevent the sale or distribution of a firearm-related product to a straw purchaser, a firearm trafficker, a person prohibited from possessing a firearm under state or federal law, or a person who the firearm industry member has reasonable cause to believe is at substantial risk of using a firearm-related product to harm themselves or unlawfully harm another or of unlawfully possessing or using a firearm-related product; (ii) prevent the loss of a firearm-related product or theft of a firearm-related product from a firearm industry member; (iii) ensure that the firearm industry member complies with all provisions of state and federal law and does not otherwise promote the unlawful manufacture, sale, possession, marketing, or use of a firearm-related product; and (iv) ensure that the firearm industry member does not engage in an act or practice in violation of the Virginia Consumer Protection Act. The bill also provides that a firearm industry member may not knowingly or recklessly create, maintain, or contribute to a public nuisance, as defined in the bill, through the sale, manufacturing, importing, or marketing of a firearm-related product. The bill creates a civil cause of action for the Attorney General or a local county or city attorney to enforce the provisions of the bill or for any person who has been injured as a result of a firearm industry member's violation to seek an injunction and to recover costs and damages.
[S.B. 515](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBU1RsF_gFgKSaZEYgmICpIxf-WNg0aLxkdXJ3mNLJ9cn0MSUBLsINTTNRwIujKZz7suq_5lH9jMpOO8cumQxQEL3DvDsImZP9FfqaXtY_OG9Aqa5RDnC9JrW7EURnB9v-lWr_RvJsQBB6z4qEKxNnrE=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Williams Graves Weapons; possession or transportation; facility that provides mental health services or developmental services; penalty. Makes it a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person to possess in or transport into any facility that provides mental health services or developmental services in the Commonwealth, including a hospital or an emergency department or other facility rendering emergency medical care, any (i) firearm or other weapon designed or intended to propel a missile or projectile of any kind; (ii) knife, except a pocket knife having a folding metal blade of less than three inches; or (iii) other dangerous weapon, including explosives and stun weapons. The bill provides that any such firearm, knife, explosive, or weapon is subject to seizure by a law-enforcement officer and specifies exceptions to the prohibition.

Requires the Commissioner of Elections to apply for, enter into, and maintain membership for the Commonwealth in the Electronic Registration Information Center (ERIC). A BILL to require the Commonwealth's participation in the Electronic Registration Information Center (ERIC).24102718D
[S.B. 642](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBUTl6F4a7HeOX2Bw4tWMXVemISkO5W4_sU2zi6TGOF00e2WcCTO0XAbGnGyHLaIPSwxvyyucy__0G-l3w-mSY_KGpIWay4GpipzpLOkBple_4VioSBwuo0xFaaDcsH2cFOZ0YkHe5LsQzAjMlTGKZ38=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Perry Purchase, possession, or transportation of firearm following an assault and battery against a person in a dating relationship with the alleged offender, penalty. Provides that any person who knowingly and intentionally purchases, possesses, or transports any firearm following a misdemeanor conviction for an offense that occurred on or after July 1, 2024, for the offense of assault and battery against a person in a dating relationship, as defined in the bill, with the alleged offender or an offense substantially similar under the laws of any other state or of the United States is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor.
[S.J.R. 14](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBS2xl_PhZZk9jt6k_UrJHVVxPIDGYjecbegwwxLiyLh0ioJRoctXJxwqG9jPxV1CLr1nZA4WWJf61v2ZDYlpaV2luPy-QCx84a0tgTFkjVlC8G5Y6lv3vp1YVajZ-JewQvE5_oTxhumXF1WLu1c_bLI=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Subramanyam Joint Commission on Technology and Science; study; advancements in artificial intelligence; report. Directs the Joint Commission on Technology and Science to study advancements in artificial intelligence (AI), including assessing (i) the impacts of deep fakes, data privacy implications, and misinformation; (ii) measures to ensure these technologies do not indirectly or directly lead to discrimination; (iii) strategies to promote equity in AI algorithms; and (iv) ways in which AI can be utilized to improve government operations and services, and to make recommendations on any appropriate legislation for consideration by the General Assembly.

Patron: Reeves Virginia Consumer Protection Act; prohibited practices; artificial intelligence disclosure. Prohibits the dissemination or sale of an item created with artificial intelligence technology that contains a videographic or still image intending to depict an actual person or an audio or audio-visual recording intending to depict the voice of an actual person where the creator has not disclosed the use of artificial intelligence technology.
[S.B. 359 ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBc02rxXJDnDidGvStjKck9uRjLfDcawasOSOcn2FsVD-K_GH8UDnacqnC061iTiQhIr753SuTjNGQ_18DLBrg_g33D0ThgAEFfQU5NBHxAjjh9BXhBwxmdom_CVSUgwwi51l-YV08Dyv1uVJf8j27MM=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==)Consumer Data Protection Act; social media platforms, addictive feed. Patron: VanValkenburg Consumer Data Protection Act; social media platforms; addictive feed. Prohibits a person that operates a social media platform that has knowledge that a user of the social media platform is a child under the age of 18 from using an addictive feed, defined in the bill, unless such social media platform obtains verifiable parental consent.
[S.B. 361 ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBUTl6F4a7HeOPylRUY_bHZEjZXl-Vtyy-o5kPP8e_YZ4vIOFx3R1rNcQ0DvgOthQXlIIQwB_pvW60UyW4pFY6HE3OTC1SLYb05glAlS6ZRe23F7euHszQLcxN01bmP7OUij1Dvevm5OvS4NOuy4obhU=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==)Consumer Data Protection Act; protections for children. Patron: VanValkenburg Consumer Data Protection Act; protections for children. Prohibits operators, defined in the bill, of websites, online services, or online or mobile applications from collecting or using the personal data of users they know are younger than the age of 18 without consent and prohibits the sale or disclosure of the personal data of such users.
[S.B. 492 ](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBc02rxXJDnDiWzLmyfolWS6e5tlAwNXH-gsmDbCrUJFpOvYy0jQaNRRCM0G6Tse-yI7K9Y-DO_3myU0NW2E7U-SQZRrcYaJBmznYSMMExG8hDoeCsmDQh__Ltsk3MKhHSx_hRbNlWYD0Pwi7sVyvU5o=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==)Va. Public Procurement Act; procurement of electric vehicles, forced and child labor prohibition. Patron: Stanley Virginia Public Procurement Act; procurement of electric vehicles; forced and child labor prohibition. Prohibits public bodies from awarding contracts to acquire an electric vehicle or electric vehicle component from a business unless such business provides a sworn declaration from the manufacturer of such electric vehicle or electric vehicle component certifying that every person involved in the production of such electric vehicle or electric vehicle component and every person involved in the sourcing, manufacturing, or mining of the material used in such electric vehicle or electric vehicle component, did not use forced labor or oppressive child labor, both terms defined in the bill, in the sourcing, manufacturing, or mining of such electric vehicle or electric vehicle component.

| | [30 minutes after session Senate Courts of Justice Committee Docket](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBS2xl_PhZZk9-_kQJ6nJ0pSmTReaWyNVamUjTDEvH7bJqMXP1jUAfm3EaXJFPtBkwDlgnWEdrt4V-_F7Qn-VaPfXbyukMegyPK8gvka26vY_99vHXG8x19yL67W38bqDINJPiIuv3OdJdIWVwrdQ46A=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==)

| [S.B. 15](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBS2xl_PhZZk9se5vZw4THp-9_iwCUKlv54uOsWGK3Szh9Svaj9T4n-4pCFL8K6xym50Tdlqd8aX3QP_lnG7Sqc5Mb7C28DOJzxyI9gfhSjzgrwrKUPBOPF8vARXcjtB9nVm3QUvjfB5EwW6pjKXF-z4=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Favola Reproductive health care services; prohibitions on extradition for certain crimes. Provides that no demand for extradition of a person charged with a criminal violation of law of another state shall be recognized by the Governor if such alleged violation involves the receipt of or assistance with reproductive health care services within the Commonwealth unless the alleged criminal violation would also constitute a criminal offense under the laws of the Commonwealth. The bill also provides that such limit on extradition shall not apply when the person who is subject to such demand for extradition by another state was physically present in the demanding state at the time of the commission of the alleged offense and thereafter fled from such state.
[S.B. 16](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBS2xl_PhZZk9k56z2-xXcwoluEQpNL3O0NJYooBgaOyajpL6QsVVbYLb8t0SR1Dr8fqwnHlczq3VO1b4GJJo-VeRE1WuNN-QWDymzjNcTTm9YSDiYOxn3Qiy2NP4bWxeez4G03NfYyqlJXe6nuouEqY=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Favola Search warrants; menstrual health data prohibited. Prohibits the issuance of a search warrant for the search and seizure of menstrual health data, as defined in the bill, stored on a computer, computer network, or other device containing electronic or digital information.
[S.B. 223](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBc02rxXJDnDiWhExwgmm906tmitUAtcivxmIQsAZF72zUWNx8DT3Kb2SAng-aqVIMTxWEnbU7E7JwXzkz8v6Eb6pQHH5pU8KN0HijUXsfFis_APqvIoHFnMlcbsbQWi8miTUJmJtqZAbFV1lGEcj-9o=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: McGuire Unlawful picketing or parading to obstruct or influence justice; penalty. Provides that any person who, with the intent of interfering with, obstructing, or impeding the administration of justice, or with the intent of influencing or intimidating in the discharge of his duty any judge, juror, witness, court officer, or court employee, pickets or parades in or near a residence occupied or used by such judge, juror, witness, court officer, or court employee is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor. The bill also provides that the provisions regarding the issuance and service of summons in place of a warrant do not apply to such violations
[S.B. 259](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBc02rxXJDnDiOpa3Kzoskd3Hc5zE-_AtVcZdUQeo-NnCASSUzBz7JeXHjXCHOMD2c0o9Rv0SFIBilfQCbJNFXVHi311oSlWPPg-Q9stYBC5UkmMJKJarXzo7tcGKuiMy5anu6GygGShV3muhfZ55rdE=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Surovell Civil actions filed on behalf of multiple persons; award of damages in action for employer retaliation. Provides that on a motion of any party, a circuit court may enter an order certifying a class or joining, coordinating, consolidating, or transferring civil actions upon finding that separate civil actions brought by a plaintiff on behalf of multiple similarly situated persons involve common questions of law or fact and arise out of the same transaction or occurrence. Currently, the circuit court may enter such order for such separate civil actions if such actions are brought by six or more plaintiffs.
[S.B. 532](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBc02rxXJDnDi-_CHql_kWXctF4H3IJER1luQemN2FwbdCbb4WXVT8wqxQRtCBTTHQg-oiVPZZ4X5n8T1ckLWRnnHZM9PJZTEQIU0pgIGdbJ_QB4km8Ip-ZyyCQo1o36sYy1403rbs2c6AsxI-Z0TBWE=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Jordan Commercial entity offering social media accounts; restricted hours for minors; civil liability. Provides that no commercial entity that offers social media accounts, as defined in the bill, shall knowingly or intentionally allow a minor to access his social media account during the hours of 12:00 a.m. to 6:00 a.m. unless the minor's parent, guardian, or legal custodian has provided permission for the minor to use such social media account during these hours. The bill provides that any commercial entity that violates these provisions shall be subject to civil liability for damages resulting from the interference with a minor's sleep cycle or mental health by allowing such minor to access to his social media account during the hours of 12:00 a.m. to 6:00 a.m. and reasonable attorney fees and costs.
[S.B. 693](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBfexu2rPNCx3u4gk8DmmrKfx_w8v5BWPRRTnw-Mht_3Zj882zcsaRx8hzBAq5ttCMIWmBZRdLfXwbSl1HPi2aCRhSVAlY_tPizlP2VMhWSQa8BFsurghJcFNKTKvY0-2Pmrgfyh5lJgezNGyTqtsrFQ=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: Sturtevant Prohibited acquisition of residential land; restrictions; civil penalty. Restricts any partnership, corporation, or real estate investment trust that manages funds pooled from investors, is a fiduciary to such investors, and has $50 million or more in net value or assets under management on any day during a taxable year from acquiring any interest in residential land, as defined in the bill, in the Commonwealth and requires registration with the Secretary of the Commonwealth on or after July 1, 2024. The bill requires the Secretary to report annually by December 1 on the registrations and any penalties assessed for failure to register and to submit such report to the Governor and the General Assembly. The bill also requires all real property tax bills to contain notice of such prohibited business ownership restrictions and registration requirements.
[S.B. 720](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001EWfJ1UbrKMYyquVTcsWXa37T8BuJmdtOIMsGayQJCnISts3LQcTVBc02rxXJDnDioVfX4kmu7C2QJyV2ZxBu64gSFxP264f_DPf2dia4ZuIArdGPAtyIAmFf9DJuZG0VRtMVHqBc4MRKZdQXuX3d_YBDbtL_9gYTGP_4CHfxH8zELMmqxnmyq5d9NJ6ynIWOfNWjyEu8HKI=&c=V2vOnMjxMcqv4VM15cmt4QMHqPupnS8VVqr_qMHiMXYh5wHTiMoqtQ==&ch=xLLV_rGfXYh7itahh2Y_Ebt_MDsT60GC9H9biWWrCdJ0WbnyBDdfDg==) Patron: McGuire Sanctuary policies prohibited. Provides that no locality shall adopt any ordinance, procedure, or policy intended to restrict the enforcement of federal immigration laws. The bill also requires an official in charge of a correctional facility to transfer custody of certain persons to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement upon receipt of a detainer. Under current law, the official may make such transfer.

Friends, YOU can participate remotely with your legislators from the comfort of your home.
YOU can watch Senate Committees in action and at the right time, comment to the committee in real time.
This is YOUR opportunity to DO SOMETHING.
Above I have listed the dockets for THIS WEDNESDAY'S Senate Committee hearings. By clicking each link you can quickly look over which bills are coming up this Wednesday that are of interest to you.
Find a bill that interests you and want to comment remotely?
Then click on the link below to the streaming of the committee meeting and follow the directions to either view the committee in real time or comment remotely to the Senate committee members in real time to state your support or opposition to a particular bill.

| Thank you to those who have pitched in to help cover my costs to communicate with you each week while the General Assembly is in session. Thank you.
Many of you have advised me that you appreciate my instructive and informative emails, regarding important bills coming before the Virginia General Assembly.
I'm delighted to continue to send you these emails and ask for your continued support so that I may continue to communicate with you.
If you haven't done so already, will you please help me offset the cost by pitching in a few dollars?
It costs about $400 per month to send out emails to keep you informed about bills coming before the General Assembly.
Would you please make a donation today to help offset the cost, so that I can continue to bring you the latest on what's going on in the General Assembly?
Thank you in advance for your continued support. After serving you for 8 years in the Virginia Senate, I know how things really work in Richmond and want to keep you informed.
Please consider donating $25, $50, $100 so that I can continue to communicate with you and help you to engage and interact with your representatives to ensure accountability and transparency.

clean_body: Participate online or in person here | | | | Senate Bills Up WEDNESDAY CALL IN REMOTELY OR SHOW UP | | Senate Committee Public Hearings for WEDNESDAY BILLS TO WATCH Wednesday's Dockets | | == | SB 491 Caroll Foy Firearm industry members; creates standards of responsible conduct, civil liability. SB 383 Deeds Firearm/explosive material, carrying w/in Capitol Square or bldg. owned or leased by the Commonwealth. SB 88 Locke Law-enforcement officers & jail officers; various changes to provisions related to decertification. SB 642 Perry Firearms; purchase, etc., following an assault and battery against person in a dating relationship. SB 327 Salim Firearms; age requirement to purchase, penalty. SB 44 VanValkenburg Owner of firearm; use of firearm by minor in an unlawful manner or to cause bodily injury. SB 515 Williams Graves Weapons; possession or transportation into a facility that provides mental health services, etc. SB 696 Williams Graves Marijuana related offenses; modification of sentence. | | == | == Patrons: VanValkenburg, Ebbin Owner of firearm; use of firearm by minor in an unlawful manner or to cause bodily injury; penalties. Creates a Class 1 misdemeanor for an owner of a firearm, as defined in the bill, who (i) allows a minor to possess such firearm or (ii) knows or reasonably should know that a minor is in close proximity, as defined in the bill, to such firearm as to allow such minor to possess or transport such firearm in violation of law and such minor (a) uses such firearm in an unlawful manner; (b) possesses such firearm within any school zone or within any building or property deemed a gun-free zone by a school board; or (c) in the case of an owner of a firearm who allows a minor to possess such firearm, uses such firearm to intentionally or with gross negligence cause bodily injury to himself or another person or in the case of an owner of a firearm who knows or reasonably should know that a minor is in close proximity to a firearm, uses such firearm to cause bodily injury to himself or another person. The bill elevates the penalty to a Class 5 felony if such owner of a firearm knows or reasonably should know that such minor has been charged with, convicted of, or adjudicated delinquent of a violent juvenile felony or has been the subject of a school-initiated threat assessment that has classified the risk posed by the minor as moderate, high, or eminent. == Patron: Locke Decertification of law-enforcement officers and jail officers. Makes various changes to the provisions related to decertification of law-enforcement officers and jail officers. The bill provides that the Department of Criminal Justice Services, rather than the Criminal Justice Services Board as provided under current law, shall oversee decertification proceedings, including receiving notice of an event requiring decertification from the sheriff, chief of police, or agency administrator or their designee, immediately decertifying a law-enforcement officer or jail officer upon receiving such notice, and initiating a review of the decertification upon request by the decertified officer. The bill specifies that the Department shall initiate such a review of the decertification in accordance with the provisions of the Administrative Process Act. The bill provides that the findings and decision of the Department may be appealed to the Board and that the final administrative decision of the Board may be then appealed and reviewed by a court. == Patron: VanValkenburg Voter registration; list maintenance data standards; challenges to a voter's registration. Prohibits the use of voter data received from another state or jurisdiction or through a list comparison for list maintenance purposes when the data file does not include a unique identifier for each individual whose information is contained in the data file. The bill requires the Department of Elections to annually audit all sources of data utilized for list maintenance activities for the purpose of determining the validity, completeness, accuracy, and reliability of the data received from each source, and to include the results of such audit in its annual report to the Committees on Privileges and Elections regarding its list maintenance activities. Lastly, the bill provides that the Circuit Court of the City of Richmond has jurisdiction over any proceeding challenging a voter's registration. The bill includes technical amendments. == Patron: Diggs Toll invoices; mail. Authorizes the use of a trackable correspondence equivalent to certified mail for sending invoices for unpaid tolls to a vehicle owner prior to the Department of Motor Vehicles placing a registration stop on such vehicle due to unpaid tolls. Current law requires such invoices to be mailed by certified mail. == Patron: Subramanyam Elections; presidential primaries; ranked choice voting. Allows political parties to hold presidential primaries using ballots that allow a voter to rank such party's candidates in his order of choice. A BILL to amend and reenact § == of the Code of Virginia and to amend the Code of Virginia by adding in Chapter 5 of Title 24.2 an article numbered 8, consisting of sections numbered == through ==, relating to elections; presidential primaries; ranked choice voting.24104872D == Patron: Salim Purchase of firearms; age requirement; penalty. Prohibits any person under 21 years of age from purchasing a firearm, with exceptions for the purchase of a rifle or shotgun by a law-enforcement officer, correctional officer, jail officer, or member of the Armed Forces of the United States, the Virginia National Guard, or the National Guard of any other state. Accordingly, the bill prohibits a licensed dealer from selling, renting, trading, or transferring from his inventory a firearm to any person under 21 years of age, with the same exceptions. A violation of either prohibition is a Class 6 felony. The bill also expands the definition of "assault firearm" as the term applies to criminal history record information checks. == Patron: Boysko Collective bargaining by public employees; labor organization representation. Repeals the existing prohibition on collective bargaining by public employees. == Patron: Deeds Carrying a firearm or explosive material within Capitol Square or building owned or leased by the Commonwealth; exemptions; public institutions of higher education; penalty. Limits the exemption from the prohibition on the carrying of any firearm or explosive material within any building owned or leased by the Commonwealth or agency thereof or any office where employees of the Commonwealth or any agency thereof are regularly present for the purpose of performing their official duties that currently applies to any property owned or operated by a public institution of higher education to instead apply to any individual within a building owned or operated by a public institution of higher education who possesses a weapon as part of such public institution of higher education's curriculum or activities or as part of any organization authorized by such public institution of higher education to conduct its programs or activities within such building. == Patron: VanValkenburg Elections; conduct of election; ranked choice voting; locally elected offices; report. Allows elections for any local or constitutional office to be conducted by ranked choice voting. Under current law, only elections of members of a county board of supervisors or a city council are allowed to be conducted by ranked choice voting. The bill also clarifies requirements for conducting elections using ranked choice voting and requires results for elections conducted by ranked choice voting to be reported along with other results reported on election night, except that such results must clearly be identified as preliminary and based on the first rankings in a ranked choice voting election. The bill provides that final tabulation for an election for a local or constitutional office that is not shared by more than one county or city is required to be conducted on the same day as other results are canvassed by the local electoral board and that final tabulation for and election for a local or constitutional office that is shared by more than one county or city is required to be conducted at a centralized facility under the supervision of the Department of Elections. The bill specifies that ranking data is required to be made publicly available by the Department and requires the State Board of Elections to provide standards and to approve vote tabulating software for use with existing voting systems in elections conducted by ranked choice voting. The bill also limits a risk-limiting audit of an election conducted using ranked choice voting to the first choice rankings reported on voting systems. Finally, the bill directs the Department to review the testing and approval framework for voting equipment in the Commonwealth and submit a report of such review no later than the first day of the 2025 Regular Session of the General Assembly. == Patron: Carroll Foy Firearm industry members; standards of responsible conduct; civil liability. Creates standards of responsible conduct for firearm industry members and requires such members to establish and implement reasonable controls regarding the manufacture, sale, distribution, use, and marketing of the firearm industry member's firearm-related products, as those terms are defined in the bill. Such reasonable controls include reasonable procedures, safeguards, and business practices that are designed to (i) prevent the sale or distribution of a firearm-related product to a straw purchaser, a firearm trafficker, a person prohibited from possessing a firearm under state or federal law, or a person who the firearm industry member has reasonable cause to believe is at substantial risk of using a firearm-related product to harm themselves or unlawfully harm another or of unlawfully possessing or using a firearm-related product; (ii) prevent the loss of a firearm-related product or theft of a firearm-related product from a firearm industry member; (iii) ensure that the firearm industry member complies with all provisions of state and federal law and does not otherwise promote the unlawful manufacture, sale, possession, marketing, or use of a firearm-related product; and (iv) ensure that the firearm industry member does not engage in an act or practice in violation of the Virginia Consumer Protection Act. The bill also provides that a firearm industry member may not knowingly or recklessly create, maintain, or contribute to a public nuisance, as defined in the bill, through the sale, manufacturing, importing, or marketing of a firearm-related product. The bill creates a civil cause of action for the Attorney General or a local county or city attorney to enforce the provisions of the bill or for any person who has been injured as a result of a firearm industry member's violation to seek an injunction and to recover costs and damages. == Patron: Williams Graves Weapons; possession or transportation; facility that provides mental health services or developmental services; penalty. Makes it a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person to possess in or transport into any facility that provides mental health services or developmental services in the Commonwealth, including a hospital or an emergency department or other facility rendering emergency medical care, any (i) firearm or other weapon designed or intended to propel a missile or projectile of any kind; (ii) knife, except a pocket knife having a folding metal blade of less than three inches; or (iii) other dangerous weapon, including explosives and stun weapons. The bill provides that any such firearm, knife, explosive, or weapon is subject to seizure by a law-enforcement officer and specifies exceptions to the prohibition. == Patron: VanValkenburg Voter registration; list maintenance; data sharing; requiring membership in the Electronic Registration Information Center (ERIC). Requires the Commissioner of Elections to apply for, enter into, and maintain membership for the Commonwealth in the Electronic Registration Information Center (ERIC). A BILL to require the Commonwealth's participation in the Electronic Registration Information Center (ERIC).24102718D == Patron: Perry Purchase, possession, or transportation of firearm following an assault and battery against a person in a dating relationship with the alleged offender, penalty. Provides that any person who knowingly and intentionally purchases, possesses, or transports any firearm following a misdemeanor conviction for an offense that occurred on or after July 1, 2024, for the offense of assault and battery against a person in a dating relationship, as defined in the bill, with the alleged offender or an offense substantially similar under the laws of any other state or of the United States is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor. == Patron: Subramanyam Joint Commission on Technology and Science; study; advancements in artificial intelligence; report. Directs the Joint Commission on Technology and Science to study advancements in artificial intelligence (AI), including assessing (i) the impacts of deep fakes, data privacy implications, and misinformation; (ii) measures to ensure these technologies do not indirectly or directly lead to discrimination; (iii) strategies to promote equity in AI algorithms; and (iv) ways in which AI can be utilized to improve government operations and services, and to make recommendations on any appropriate legislation for consideration by the General Assembly. | | == | | == | Consumer Protection Act; prohibited practices, artificial intelligence disclosure. Patron: Reeves Virginia Consumer Protection Act; prohibited practices; artificial intelligence disclosure. Prohibits the dissemination or sale of an item created with artificial intelligence technology that contains a videographic or still image intending to depict an actual person or an audio or audio-visual recording intending to depict the voice of an actual person where the creator has not disclosed the use of artificial intelligence technology. Data Protection Act; social media platforms, addictive feed. Patron: VanValkenburg Consumer Data Protection Act; social media platforms; addictive feed. Prohibits a person that operates a social media platform that has knowledge that a user of the social media platform is a child under the age of 18 from using an addictive feed, defined in the bill, unless such social media platform obtains verifiable parental consent. Data Protection Act; protections for children. Patron: VanValkenburg Consumer Data Protection Act; protections for children. Prohibits operators, defined in the bill, of websites, online services, or online or mobile applications from collecting or using the personal data of users they know are younger than the age of 18 without consent and prohibits the sale or disclosure of the personal data of such users. . Public Procurement Act; procurement of electric vehicles, forced and child labor prohibition. Patron: Stanley Virginia Public Procurement Act; procurement of electric vehicles; forced and child labor prohibition. Prohibits public bodies from awarding contracts to acquire an electric vehicle or electric vehicle component from a business unless such business provides a sworn declaration from the manufacturer of such electric vehicle or electric vehicle component certifying that every person involved in the production of such electric vehicle or electric vehicle component and every person involved in the sourcing, manufacturing, or mining of the material used in such electric vehicle or electric vehicle component, did not use forced labor or oppressive child labor, both terms defined in the bill, in the sourcing, manufacturing, or mining of such electric vehicle or electric vehicle component. | | == | == Patron: Favola Reproductive health care services; prohibitions on extradition for certain crimes. Provides that no demand for extradition of a person charged with a criminal violation of law of another state shall be recognized by the Governor if such alleged violation involves the receipt of or assistance with reproductive health care services within the Commonwealth unless the alleged criminal violation would also constitute a criminal offense under the laws of the Commonwealth. The bill also provides that such limit on extradition shall not apply when the person who is subject to such demand for extradition by another state was physically present in the demanding state at the time of the commission of the alleged offense and thereafter fled from such state. == Patron: Favola Search warrants; menstrual health data prohibited. Prohibits the issuance of a search warrant for the search and seizure of menstrual health data, as defined in the bill, stored on a computer, computer network, or other device containing electronic or digital information. == Patron: McGuire Unlawful picketing or parading to obstruct or influence justice; penalty. Provides that any person who, with the intent of interfering with, obstructing, or impeding the administration of justice, or with the intent of influencing or intimidating in the discharge of his duty any judge, juror, witness, court officer, or court employee, pickets or parades in or near a residence occupied or used by such judge, juror, witness, court officer, or court employee is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor. The bill also provides that the provisions regarding the issuance and service of summons in place of a warrant do not apply to such violations == Patron: Surovell Civil actions filed on behalf of multiple persons; award of damages in action for employer retaliation. Provides that on a motion of any party, a circuit court may enter an order certifying a class or joining, coordinating, consolidating, or transferring civil actions upon finding that separate civil actions brought by a plaintiff on behalf of multiple similarly situated persons involve common questions of law or fact and arise out of the same transaction or occurrence. Currently, the circuit court may enter such order for such separate civil actions if such actions are brought by six or more plaintiffs. == Patron: Jordan Commercial entity offering social media accounts; restricted hours for minors; civil liability. Provides that no commercial entity that offers social media accounts, as defined in the bill, shall knowingly or intentionally allow a minor to access his social media account during the hours of 12:00 a.m. to 6:00 a.m. unless the minor's parent, guardian, or legal custodian has provided permission for the minor to use such social media account during these hours. The bill provides that any commercial entity that violates these provisions shall be subject to civil liability for damages resulting from the interference with a minor's sleep cycle or mental health by allowing such minor to access to his social media account during the hours of 12:00 a.m. to 6:00 a.m. and reasonable attorney fees and costs. == Patron: Sturtevant Prohibited acquisition of residential land; restrictions; civil penalty. Restricts any partnership, corporation, or real estate investment trust that manages funds pooled from investors, is a fiduciary to such investors, and has $50 million or more in net value or assets under management on any day during a taxable year from acquiring any interest in residential land, as defined in the bill, in the Commonwealth and requires registration with the Secretary of the Commonwealth on or after July 1, 2024. The bill requires the Secretary to report annually by December 1 on the registrations and any penalties assessed for failure to register and to submit such report to the Governor and the General Assembly. The bill also requires all real property tax bills to contain notice of such prohibited business ownership restrictions and registration requirements. == Patron: McGuire Sanctuary policies prohibited. Provides that no locality shall adopt any ordinance, procedure, or policy intended to restrict the enforcement of federal immigration laws. The bill also requires an official in charge of a correctional facility to transfer custody of certain persons to U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement upon receipt of a detainer. Under current law, the official may make such transfer. | == | | ACTION, ACTION, ACTION | Friends, YOU can participate remotely with your legislators from the comfort of your home. YOU can watch Senate Committees in action and at the right time, comment to the committee in real time. This is YOUR opportunity to DO SOMETHING. Above I have listed the dockets for THIS WEDNESDAY'S Senate Committee hearings. By clicking each link you can quickly look over which bills are coming up this Wednesday that are of interest to you. Find a bill that interests you and want to comment remotely? Then click on the link below to the streaming of the committee meeting and follow the directions to either view the committee in real time or comment remotely to the Senate committee members in real time to state your support or opposition to a particular bill. It's that easy to express your support or opposition to a bill coming up on this Wednesday's docket. | | GET REGULAR UPDATES | Thank you to those who have pitched in to help cover my costs to communicate with you each week while the General Assembly is in session. Thank you. Many of you have advised me that you appreciate my instructive and informative emails, regarding important bills coming before the Virginia General Assembly. I'm delighted to continue to send you these emails and ask for your continued support so that I may continue to communicate with you. If you haven't done so already, will you please help me offset the cost by pitching in a few dollars? It costs about $400 per month to send out emails to keep you informed about bills coming before the General Assembly. Would you please make a donation today to help offset the cost, so that I can continue to bring you the latest on what's going on in the General Assembly? Thank you in advance for your continued support. After serving you for 8 years in the Virginia Senate, I know how things really work in Richmond and want to keep you informed. Please consider donating $25, $50, $100 so that I can continue to communicate with you and help you to engage and interact with your representatives to ensure accountability and transparency. | | == | | | | | | | | | == | | == | | == | | == | == | | == | | == | | == | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | | | | | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. Box 5811, Midlothian, VA 23112 | | Sent by mailto:amanda@senatoramandachase.ccsend.com powered by

Here’s the thing: these issues aren’t just political talking points for Mondaire. They are a call to action.

Mondaire is ready to:
* Pass the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act to **end discriminatory voting practices** that make it harder for Black and brown communities to cast a ballot.
* Build on the Inflation Reduction Act to cut emissions, invest in renewable energy, and **save our planet.**
* Protect access to abortion through the Women’s Health Protection Act so everyone can make their own health care decisions.
* Put the lives of our children over the NRA to enact**commonsense gun safety reforms,** including universal background checks and a ban on assault weapons.

[**These policies aren’t a pipe dream, but the only way to make them a reality is by flipping NY-17 and retaking our House majority. Chip in any amount to get Mondaire back to Congress and let’s get it done together.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78530298/453666049/-460793036?refcode=em24_240206_SurRes_fndd_gq_act___TM_ettx&utm_campaign=em24_240206_SurRes_fndd_gq_act___TM_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=bcfed144-3fc5-ee11-b660-002248223197&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODAzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiY2ZlZDE0NC0zZmM1LWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjMxOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rQ5-DsixU_kmnBPwgBePXJoBIj0K3D_gOXbzmk8F8qQ=)

Thanks again, team.

Mondaire for Congress

Mondaire Jones is running for Congress to finish the work he started last term. He’s ready to fight to lower costs for residents of the Hudson Valley, defend our democracy, raise wages, and protect reproductive rights for all. And he’s doing it without a penny from corporate PACs. clean_body: Here’s what you had to say: Team—Last month, we sent you a quick survey so that Mondaire can hear directly from you about the issues at the top of your mind. Thank you for taking the time to complete our 2024 Priorities Survey and help us better understand how Mondaire can best serve your interests in Congress. The results are in! Now, here’s what you had to say: | = Mondaire has heard you loud and clear—and he’s prepared to tackle these issues immediately in Congress. But first, we need to ensure he has what it takes to get there. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Here’s the thing: these issues aren’t just political talking points for Mondaire. They are a call to action. Mondaire is ready to: Pass the John Lewis Voting Rights Advancement Act to end discriminatory voting practices that make it harder for Black and brown communities to cast a ballot. Build on the Inflation Reduction Act to cut emissions, invest in renewable energy, and save our planet. Protect access to abortion through the Women’s Health Protection Act so everyone can make their own health care decisions. Put the lives of our children over the NRA to enactcommonsense gun safety reforms, including universal background checks and a ban on assault weapons. = Thanks again, team. Mondaire for Congress Mondaire Jones is running for Congress to finish the work he started last term. He’s ready to fight to lower costs for residents of the Hudson Valley, defend our democracy, raise wages, and protect reproductive rights for all. And he’s doing it without a penny from corporate PACs. = = = ** ** PAID FOR BY MONDAIRE FOR CONGRESS P.O. BOX #873, SLEEPY HOLLOW, NY 10591

name: Andrew (via FFR)
subject: No Labels is one of the biggest threats to our country
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided in kind by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| [ ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/26910?t=1&akid=20733%2E4449941%2ERk2DKv)
---
Hey,

You’ve probably heard about No Labels over the past few months. They claim to support bipartisanship and working across the aisle, but in reality, it’s just a scheme for conservative donors and corporations to contribute massive amounts of money anonymously and to help elect Donald Trump again.

_It was even exposed that they have connections with major right-wing donors like David Koch and Harlan Crow._ **In a time period of hyper partisanship, they’re preying on voters’ desires for unity and meaningful progress.** [**We can band together to stop them, but we need the support of Democrats like you, who want to see the corruption in our political system brought to an end. Will you chip in $7 to help us combat Dark Money groups like No Labels?**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/36955?t=7&akid=20733%2E4449941%2ERk2DKv) | [DONATE »](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/36955?t=8&akid=20733%2E4449941%2ERk2DKv)
---
**Let me be very clear:** No Labels is a corrupt political organization that is fighting to continue the destructive trend of executives and corporations lining their pockets at the expense of American families.

The kind of damage that a Dark Money organization like this can inflict without being held accountable is immense, and we have to put a stop to it.

We founded our organization to fight the influence of Dark Money on our political system – we know the only way to take special interests down is to assemble a vast people-powered movement.

**That’s where you come in. We both know special interests have huge amounts of resources to burn in an effort to stop us.**

That’s why we need you on our team. Together, we can create a political system that is TRULY controlled by the American people, not just the most wealthy and well-connected.
[**So I’m asking, now that you’ve read a bit about No Label’s corruption and our mission here at Fight for Reform, if you’d support our movement »**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/36955?t=9&akid=20733%2E4449941%2ERk2DKv)
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
[CONTRIBUTE $7 »](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/36956?t=10&akid=20733%2E4449941%2ERk2DKv)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/36957?t=11&akid=20733%2E4449941%2ERk2DKv)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/36958?t=12&akid=20733%2E4449941%2ERk2DKv)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/36959?t=13&akid=20733%2E4449941%2ERk2DKv)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/36955?t=14&akid=20733%2E4449941%2ERk2DKv)
Together we can make a huge change,

– Andrew

name: =?utf-8?Q?Suzanne=20Crouch?=
subject: I'm calling for an audit of the FSSA as budget cuts threaten vulnerable Hoosier families
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Suzanne Crouch
body:
The audit is necessary in the wake of the staggering $1B Medicaid overspend and proposed budget cuts to Indiana's attendant care program.

https://www.crouchforindiana.com/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

** TODAY: I️'m calling for an independent, outside audit of the FSSA (https://indianacapitalchronicle.com/briefs/crouch-calls-for-fssa-audit-following-1b-shortfall-medicaid-changes/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
------------------------------------------------------------

Friends and fellow Hoosiers,

Today, I️ called for an independent, outside audit of the Family and Social Services Administration (FSSA) to ensure accountability and transparency following the $1 billion Medicaid overspend and to ensure that such egregious errors do not reoccur.

This failure cannot go unexamined, especially when it threatens critical programs (https://www.wthr.com/article/news/local/indiana/parents-protest-impending-fssa-cuts-indiana-statehouse-caregiver-assistance/531-6426584f-3648-4af2-9208-24b1e9b2a84d?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) designed to support our most vulnerable citizens, particularly medically complex children.
https://indianacapitalchronicle.com/briefs/crouch-calls-for-fssa-audit-following-1b-shortfall-medicaid-changes/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Joined by South Bend mother Melanie Kandzierski and her 3-year-old daughter Rosie, I emphasized the urgency of preserving the attendant care program (https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&rct=j&q=&esrc=s&source=newssearch&cd=&cad=rja&uact=8&ved=2ahUKEwjcuZKlu5eEAxXDl4kEHYLbBgEQxfQBKAB6BAgOEAE&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwgntv.com%2Fnorthwest-indiana%2Fgoing-to-devastate-them-indiana-drastically-changing-program-that-pays-parents-to-care-for-children-with-disabilities%2F&usg=AOvVaw2PQams83czyR5jF1rNjBuJ&opi=89978449&utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) for parents of medically complex children in Indiana.

During the press conference (https://indianacapitalchronicle.com/briefs/crouch-calls-for-fssa-audit-following-1b-shortfall-medicaid-changes/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) , Kandzierski shared her family's story, shedding light on the indispensable role of attendant care for Legally Responsible Individuals. Their tireless dedication to caring for Rosie, who suffers from a rare seizure disorder, highlights the critical importance of the attendant care program in our state.

We cannot allow bureaucratic mistakes compromise the quality of life and care for children like Rosie.
https://dailyjournal.net/2024/02/06/crouch-calls-for-fssa-audit-following-1b-shortfall-medicaid-changes/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

The FSSA's proposed pivot to Structured Family Caregiving falls short of adequately addressing the comprehensive needs of these children. We cannot accept band-aid solutions when the lives of our most vulnerable are at stake.

Last week, I️ called on the FSSA to pause its proposed cuts, and I️ implored the General Assembly to support the pause of these cuts.
https://indianacapitalchronicle.com/2024/01/29/lt-gov-lawmakers-call-on-fssa-to-pause-medicaid-cuts-for-parents-of-disabled-children/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

As we move forward, I urge all Hoosiers to join me in demanding accountability (https://www.am1050.com/2024/02/05/crouch-urges-hoosiers-to-contact-legislators-regarding-fssa-cuts/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) from FSSA and ensuring that critical programs remain intact for those who need them most.

Contact your legislators (https://www.thecouriertimes.com/government/crouch-urges-hoosiers-to-contact-legislators-regarding-fssa-cuts/article_10027f43-e2ae-5b5e-a61d-a43a8d2b4aed.html?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) , raise your voice, and demand that these cuts be paused until we receive the answers and assurances necessary to guarantee the care our medically complex children deserve.
https://readthereporter.com/lt-gov-crouch-urges-hoosiers-to-contact-their-state-legislators-regarding-fssa-cuts/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Hoosier families deserve answers, and it is our duty to provide them with the transparency and accountability they rightfully demand.

Let us stand united in defense of compassion, empathy, and the well-being of our fellow Hoosiers.

In solidarity,

Let's keep the conversation going! Follow me on social to keep up with the campaign. Have questions, comments or concerns? Send me an email!
https://www.facebook.com/CrouchForIndiana?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/CrouchForIndiana?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://twitter.com/Suzanne_Crouch?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D Twitter (https://twitter.com/Suzanne_Crouch?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.instagram.com/crouchforindiana/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D Instagram (https://www.instagram.com/crouchforindiana/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://crouchforindiana.com?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D Website (https://crouchforindiana.com?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
mailto:info@crouchforindiana.com Email (mailto:info@crouchforindiana.com)

https://vimeo.com/780244425?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=ccac3d874d-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2024_02_06_07_04&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-ccac3d874d-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

name: Amelia Salinas
subject: Nice to meet you 👋, I’m Amelia
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrea Salinas for Oregon
body:
You may know Andrea Salinas as a Congresswoman, a proud Oregonian, or a champion for women and working families. But I just know her as Mother.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78530638/453666979/1032217744?refcode=em_fr_2024.02.06_b1_as_all-nd&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZTlmYTQ0MS00MGM1LWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjMxOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Urj9I4FGCCnHMpxXKu30t58iapEsYMOdyx0snewigwY=)
---
**You may know Andrea Salinas as a Congresswoman, a proud Oregonian, or a champion for women and working families. But I just know her as Mother.** Hi there – I’m Amelia, and I’m Andrea’s daughter! [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78530639/453666980/1032217744?refcode=em_fr_2024.02.06_b1_as_all-nd&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZTlmYTQ0MS00MGM1LWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjMxOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Urj9I4FGCCnHMpxXKu30t58iapEsYMOdyx0snewigwY=) Anyone who knows my mom knows that she’s the real deal. Each and every day, she goes to work ready to fight for every family in Oregon. I’ll never forget my junior year of high school when the _Dobbs_ decision came out. My friends and I were devastated to hear the news and we knew that our freedoms and futures were on the line. I came home and told my mom that this decision will determine where I go to college. But for some, that Supreme Court decision could alter the course of their entire life. It was comforting to know that my mom had been a champion for choice her entire life and protected my freedom to access abortion care as a state legislator. In the Oregon State Legislature, she championed efforts to pass the nation’s most progressive paid family leave bill. She fought for more accessible, affordable quality health care. She introduced and passed a bill that made Big Pharma accountable to consumers and led to lower prescription drug prices. _The list goes on and on_. **And she’s done it all with honesty, integrity, and compassion._Because that’s who she is._** **Look, when my mom was deciding whether to run for Congress in 2021, one of her biggest motivations was her commitment to fighting for a better world for _me_ and my generation.** She knew she couldn’t sit on the sidelines and watch our climate change, our democracy decay, and our bodily autonomy get stripped away. Change isn’t easy, but my mom has never let that stop her. She’s fighting hard in DC, but there’s so much more work to be done. **[That’s why I’m asking for my mom – Will you support her re-election campaign? Her race is one of the most competitive in the country, so we’ll need your help to ensure she can keep fighting for Oregon?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78530640/453666981/1032217744?refcode=em_fr_2024.02.06_b1_as_all-nd&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZTlmYTQ0MS00MGM1LWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjMxOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Urj9I4FGCCnHMpxXKu30t58iapEsYMOdyx0snewigwY=) ** | _[Donate>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78530641/453666982/1032217744?refcode=em_fr_2024.02.06_b1_as_all-nd&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmZTlmYTQ0MS00MGM1LWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjMxOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Urj9I4FGCCnHMpxXKu30t58iapEsYMOdyx0snewigwY=)_
---
Thank you so much,

Amelia
** **

name: Carmen Goers
subject: Celebrate National Girls & Women in Sports Day with Us
body:
Join the Team

| | | | | | | |
---
| | | |
---
| | | **Celebrate National Girls & Women in Sports Day with Us**
---

Dear Peter,
Tomorrow, as we celebrate National Girls & Women in Sports Day, our campaign in Washington's 8th Congressional District is proud to honor the incredible achievements of female athletes and reaffirm our commitment to fostering equality and opportunity in sports.
All of my girls played some kind of sport growing up, whether it was volleyball, basketball or softball. Women and girls in sports is a great thing. However, this is under attack by those that want to play in women’s sports but were not born a girl.
This special day is not just about recognizing the monumental accomplishments of women and girls in athletics; it's about inspiring and **protecting** the next generation of leaders and champions. It's a call to action to support policies and initiatives that ensure every girl has the chance to play, compete, and succeed in the sports they love.

---
| | | |
---
|
Would you consider contributing $20, $50, or $100? As your representative, I will:

* Ensure the protection of female-only spaces on the field and in the locker room.
* Support parental rights to protect their own children.
* Insist that school sport programs focus on sport and fair play, not indoctrination into specific ideologies

---
| [ DONATE TODAY! ](https://vgq.soundestlink.com/link/65c2b66d6d3f4d908cbdfcfe/65c171f3fd5b808461765292/652845d9aa222127cf458b43?signature=45d00222981ee6d2859a8e1866e7f59c8ab591ff6d1edfd9b163edeb76c01d6d)
---
| | | Let's work together to build a future where every girl can achieve her sporting dreams without limitation or danger. Your donation today is a step toward that future.

Thank you for standing with us.

Sincerely,

Carmen Goers

---
|
---
| | | | [](https://vgq.soundestlink.com/link/65c2b66d6d3f4d908cbdfd00/65c171f3fd5b808461765292/652845d9aa222127cf458b43?signature=45d00222981ee6d2859a8e1866e7f59c8ab591ff6d1edfd9b163edeb76c01d6d) | [](https://vgq.soundestlink.com/link/65c2b66d6d3f4d908cbdfd02/65c171f3fd5b808461765292/652845d9aa222127cf458b43?signature=45d00222981ee6d2859a8e1866e7f59c8ab591ff6d1edfd9b163edeb76c01d6d)

name: John Fetterman
subject: *Seriously* scary stuff
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
| My senior Senator and friend Bob Casey is running for re-election!!!
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=1968%2E35263%2EdoYyAk)
Hey Peter, it’s John. I really need to talk to you about my friend Bob Casey. The 2024 election is here — and **the GOP’s road to winning back the Senate runs right through PA.** Republicans are zeroing in on 2024’s battleground states as we speak. You can bet Mitch McConnell and the GOP’s shadiest billionaires will spend hundreds of million$ to attack Bob. This is *seriously* scary stuff, but you know what gives ME hope? This amazing grassroots team!!! The way we stopped the GOP in 2022 is the same way we fight back and re-elect Bob now: by bringing together an ✨all-star grassroots team✨ that can stand up to the GOP’s money by folks chipping in $5, $25, or more at a time. Together, we’ll defend PA’s Senate seat, as long as we don’t lose sight of the stakes. **Holding this seat is make-or-break for our Democratic majority.** [**With that in mind... please, Peter, split $10 or more between Bob’s campaign and Tammy Baldwin. We need to build a winning campaign between now and Election Day, so I’m asking for yinz guys to dig deep!**](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2155?c0=tbe-em-casey.PWALcl&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20240206_PM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=1968%2E35263%2EdoYyAk) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Bob Casey and Tammy Baldwin will go through immediately:_ | | [SPLIT $10 NOW](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2151?c0=tbe-em-casey.kD9PQC&refcode=AU_STB_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20240206_PM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=1968%2E35263%2EdoYyAk)

name: Kotek HQ
subject: The 2024 legislative session is off and sprinting!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek
body:
|
#
---
Peter — Oregon legislators are off and sprinting in their 35-day session. Governor Kotek always works to put Oregonians’ priorities first, and that’s why her top focus this session is to create more housing for everyone, for every community. Her housing production legislation — **the only bill she introduced this year** — seeks to allocate $500 million for local infrastructure, workforce housing, energy efficiency construction, and more – while also adding affordability requirements to make housing more affordable across the state. [_**So, will you add your name to show your support for a productive legislative session? clean_body: | Peter — Oregon legislators are off and sprinting in their 35-day session. Governor Kotek always works to put Oregonians’ priorities first, and that’s why her top focus this session is to create more housing for everyone, for every community. Her housing production legislation — the only bill she introduced this year — seeks to allocate $500 million for local infrastructure, workforce housing, energy efficiency construction, and more – while also adding affordability requirements to make housing more affordable across the state. | Thank you for showing your support to get things done! Kotek HQ Tina for Oregon PO Box 42307 Portland, OR 97242 | mailto:info@tinafororegon.com | Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek

name: Juliana at United We Dream Action
subject: This bill is horrific.
body:
The bill closes our border and guts our asylum system. | | | This is **URGENT** , Peter: The Senate released their supplemental funding bill this week, and if it passed, the consequences will be **horrific.** The bill includes record amounts of detention beds and border patrol agents and changes to our border policy that would deny migrants the human right to seek asylum — all to fund war and genocide abroad. Immigrants are once again being used as political pawns, and we’re running out of time to stop this bill from becoming law, and**seeing our border effectively closed and our asylum system gutted.** **The White House is pressuring Congress to finalize this deal. We must act now before it’s too late. name: Emilia Sykes
subject: In celebration and solidarity: Happy Black History Month.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emilia Sykes for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78530796/453667630/-1657784636?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NTEyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MWFlNzlhNi00MmM1LWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjMxOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=v49GUhNd2RaWQRe7E7EIH9ZUtALimO8V8iEKv_coL1s=&emci=47f8b48c-17c3-ee11-b660-002248223197&emdi=61ae79a6-42c5-ee11-b660-002248223197&ceid=11703046) Peter,

In the midst of this busy political campaign and historic election year, I want to take a moment to celebrate Black History Month and uplift the stories of the countless Black Americans who have helped to shape our nation and create a more perfect union — especially those right here in Ohio’s 13th Congressional District.

As we celebrate, we must also be mindful that our fight for equality and justice can’t begin — nor end — in Black History Month. **This month should serve as a proud reminder of how far we’ve come, and a call to action for the work we still must do all year long.**

Looking ahead, we must build on the legacies of those who came before us — those who opened doors, shattered glass ceilings, and proved the breadth of Black excellence.

And we must never forget that it is thanks to the tireless work and fearless advocacy of Black leaders, like Shirley Chisholm and Stephanie Tubbs Jones, that a Black woman like me is able to serve as a member of the United States Congress. _I am honored to follow in their footsteps as the first Black person elected to represent Ohio’s 13th Congressional District._

So to every Black community leader, artist, small business owner, and revolutionary who has helped make this nation a better place: **_thank you._**

In celebration and solidarity: Happy Black History Month.

_Emilia_
---
| Emilia Sykes is running for re-election in Ohio's 13th Congressional District. To learn more, please visit [EmiliaSykesForCongress.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78530797/453667631/-1657784636?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NTEyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MWFlNzlhNi00MmM1LWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjMxOTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=v49GUhNd2RaWQRe7E7EIH9ZUtALimO8V8iEKv_coL1s=&emci=47f8b48c-17c3-ee11-b660-002248223197&emdi=61ae79a6-42c5-ee11-b660-002248223197&ceid=11703046)
